---
title: 'catalog run-cached'
---

Same semantics as [`catalog run`](run.qmd), but wraps the resulting expression
with a `ParquetCache` so subsequent invocations short-circuit when inputs
haven't changed.

## Usage

```bash
xorq catalog run-cached <entries...> [OPTIONS]
```

## Arguments

- **`entries`**—One or more entry names or aliases.

## Options

All of [`catalog run`](run.qmd)'s options are accepted. The cache-specific options:

| Option | Default | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `--cache-dir` | `$XORQ_CACHE_DIR` or `~/.cache/xorq` | Directory for parquet cache files. |
| `--cache-type` | `modification-time` | Cache strategy. One of `modification-time` (`ParquetCache`) or `snapshot` (`ParquetSnapshotCache`). |
| `--ttl` | none | TTL in seconds for snapshot cache. When set, uses `ParquetTTLSnapshotCache` instead. |

See [`run-cached`](../run-cached.qmd) for a description of the cache strategies.

## Examples

Default cache (modification-time):
```bash
xorq catalog run-cached src trn \
    --cache-dir ./cache \
    -o results.parquet
```

Snapshot cache with TTL:
```bash
xorq catalog run-cached pipeline \
    --cache-type snapshot \
    --ttl 3600 \
    --cache-dir ./cache \
    -o results.parquet
```
